Troy SD Check Register Reconciliation

All disbursements FY11-FY26, classified by Michigan PSAM (Bulletin 1022) function codes with subject sub-classification on instructional spending. 224,267 line items, $1227M total — split into operating ($774M) and bond/capital ($453M) views.

Source: Troy SD BoardDocs · 152 monthly check register PDFs · FY26 partial (through April 2026)

Years with material under-coverage (potential undercount of curriculum spend)
  • FY11 — 4 of 12 months (corpus starts Jan 2011; missing Jul-Dec 2010 + Feb/May 2011). Treat FY11 as a partial-year stub.
  • FY20 — 5 of 12 months. The BoardDocs transition window (Jul 2019, Jan-Jun 2020 missing). The first AVMR / Math Recovery materials show here but the picture is genuinely partial.
  • FY11-FY26 — 2-4 months missing each year. Means the FY13 K-5 ELA adoption spike ($924K) and the FY14 K-5 Math + 6-12 Math adoption spike ($697K + $276K) might still be undercounted if the missing months happened to include large materials buys.

Math & ELA curriculum spend · FY11-FY26 spotlight

Materials + PD spend by curriculum swimlane, pivoted on transaction date (true FY). Each bar is split into curriculum/materials (darker shade) and professional development (lighter shade — function code 221 + PD-only vendors like TCRWP, AVMR, Math Recovery). Two clear adoption cycles ~10 years apart: 2012-2014 (HMH-era, materials-heavy) and 2022-2025 (Bridges/IM/TCRWP refresh era, PD-heavy).

Annual spend by swimlane ($ thousands)

Pivoted on Issue Date FY (true transaction year). Bold rows = adoption-spike years.

Fiscal Year K-5 ELA K-5 Math 6-12 ELA 6-12 Math Notable

Two adoption cycles, ~10 years apart

Cycle 1 · 2012-2014 (HMH era)
  • K-5 ELA spike FY13 ($924K) — HMH Journeys + workshop model, May 2012 board action
  • K-5 Math spike FY14 ($697K) — HMH Math Expressions, May 2013 board action
  • 6-12 Math spike FY14-15 ($469K) — HMH Larson Big Ideas, May 2013
  • 6-12 ELA stayed flat ($80K total across all 4 years) — no secondary ELA refresh in this cycle
  • Then 5 years near-zero K-5 math spend (FY11-FY26)
Cycle 2 · 2022-2025 (next-gen era)
  • K-5 Math spike FY23-24 ($1.12M) — Bridges + AVMR, Mar 2023
  • 6-12 Math ramp FY23-26 ($782K) — Illustrative Math, May 2023
  • K-5 ELA sustained $290K/yr — TCRWP baseline + May 2025 Writing Units refresh
  • 6-12 ELA $696K — steady ~$100K/yr Turnitin/CommonLit/EMC, no big adoption
  • Cycle is ~$5.9M and still ramping (vs Cycle 1 total of ~$2.5M)

Period totals

Period K-5 ELA K-5 Math 6-12 ELA 6-12 Math Combined
Cycle 1 (FY11-FY26)$968K$722K$80K$746K$2.52M
Steady-state (FY11-FY26)$1.11M$40K$790K$54K$1.99M
Cycle 2 (FY11-FY26)$1.67M$2.69M$696K$885K$5.94M
All-time total$3.75M$3.46M$1.57M$1.69M$10.46M

Key takeaways

  • Cycle 2 is already 2.4× the size of Cycle 1 — $5.9M (still ramping) vs $2.5M for the entire 2012-2014 wave. K-5 Math investment per cycle has nearly tripled ($722K → $2.69M), partly real expansion (intervention infrastructure didn't exist in 2014), partly inflation, partly more PD-heavy approach.
  • K-5 ELA is the most consistent line — even during the FY11-FY26 "steady state" the district spent $250-500K/year on TCRWP workshop-model PD with no new materials. This is a permanent investment line, not just adoption-driven.
  • 6-12 ELA peaked off-cycle in FY11-FY26 ($620K) rather than tracking either named cycle — looks like a one-time HS English textbook/Turnitin/CommonLit refresh that didn't come with a public board adoption motion.
  • Secondary swimlanes are materials-heavy, K-5 is PD-heavy — 6-12 ELA is only 11% PD and 6-12 Math is 3% PD, vs K-5 ELA 49% PD and K-5 Math 46% PD. Elementary instruction is where the district invests in coaching/training; secondary is mostly textbook/platform purchases.
  • 6-12 Math is the lightest math swimlane ($1.69M over 16 years — about half the K-5 lines). Reflects thinner secondary-level PD investment and the longer life of HS textbook adoptions.
  • FY11-FY26 K-5 Math near zero ($94 + $1,385) — the gap right before AVMR/Math Recovery showed up suggests minimal math PD investment between the 2014 adoption settling and the 2020 intervention scale-up.
  • FY12 6-12 Math at $267K before any board action — pilot/preview activity for Big Ideas Math, May 2013 adoption.

Data coverage gaps (months without a check register in the source corpus)

Where a register PDF is missing from BoardDocs, the corresponding month's curriculum spend is invisible. Years with bigger gaps may understate the true totals shown above. Treat partial-year FYs accordingly.

FY Months captured Specific months missing Curriculum-analysis risk

FY138 of 12Jul/Sep 2012, Mar/Jun 2013K-5 ELA adoption-spike year — $924K likely understated
FY148 of 12Jul/Aug/Oct 2013, Jun 2014K-5 Math + 6-12 Math adoption — could miss materials
FY159 of 12Jul/Sep 2014, Feb 2015Math rollout tail
FY1610 of 12Jul/Sep 2015Steady-state — minor risk
FY1712 of 12 ✅—Complete
FY1810 of 12Jul 2017, Jun 2018TCRWP era — minor risk
FY1911 of 12Jul 2018Minor
FY205 of 12Jul 2019, Jan-Jun 2020Major undercount — COVID-era spend largely invisible
FY2110 of 12Jul 2020, Jan 2021Minor — early AVMR ramp may be partial
FY2212 of 12 ✅—Complete
FY2312 of 12 ✅—Complete — Bridges + IM adoption fully captured
FY2411 of 12Nov 2023Mid-Year 1 of new K-5 Math adoption — minor risk
FY2512 of 12 ✅—Complete
FY2610 of 12May/Jun 2026 (not yet held)In progress — by design

Two missing-data root causes: (1) The check-register PDF was never attached to the BoardDocs meeting — the meeting agenda only listed the Treasurer's Report as a consent-agenda item with the register as an external handout that didn't make the digital record. (2) The meeting itself was not held that month (rare). See Working Folder/Cache and Tools/project_docs/INDEX.md for the full audit, root-cause analysis, and recovery history (including a bundle-folder date-attribution bug fixed May 2026 and a pypdf fallback that recovered Aug 2012 + Oct 2019 registers from PDFs that pdfplumber couldn't read).

Note: this swimlane analysis includes major curriculum vendors only (TCRWP, Heinemann, Greenwood/F&P, HMH, Math Learning Center, US Math Recovery, Imagine Learning IM, Cengage Big Ideas, Agile Mind, Open Up Resources, etc.). Excludes general supply purchases and non-curriculum vendors. See the full spending breakdown below for the complete picture.

Operating expenditures non-bond

General Fund, Special Education Fund, Food Service Fund, Student Activity Fund, Latchkey/Childcare Fund, Athletics Fund. Excludes bond construction, sinking fund, and debt service. This is the budget that runs the schools day-to-day.

Bond, sinking fund, and debt expenditures bond + capital + debt

Capital Projects (Bond) funds, Building & Site Sinking Fund (millage-funded facility maintenance), and Debt Service Fund (interest payments on outstanding bond debt). This is the facilities and capital side of the budget — driven by the 2023 bond program currently under construction.

Methodology

Source PDFs were parsed via Pentamation table extraction. Each line was tagged with its Michigan PSAM Function Code per MDE Bulletin 1022. For instructional functions (110-119, 221-229), a rule-based subject classifier assigned tags from vendor name + description text. The 32 PSAM categories aggregate to the standard 4-digit chart of accounts. Lines with blank function codes (typically payroll batches and clearing transactions) are categorized by description.

Operating vs Bond split: "Operating" includes General Fund, Special Education Fund, Food Service, Student Activity, Latchkey/Childcare, and Athletics funds. "Bond/capital" includes Capital Projects Bond funds (419, 423, 498), Building & Site Sinking Fund (314-323), and Debt Service Fund (511). Operating + Bond reconciles exactly to All ($203M + $214M = $417M).

Important interpretation note: "Support — Instructional Staff (PD/Curriculum)" Function 221 totals include both genuine curriculum/PD spending AND technology refreshes (iPads, MacBooks, network gear) that some districts code under 221. Apple-related vendors alone account for ~40% of Function 221 spending in this dataset. Use the vendor drill-down to separate.

Source data

The full master workbook used to compile this dashboard is available for download: Troy_SD_Check_Register_FY11-FY26.xlsx (7 sheets: README, All Lines (47,918 rows), By Year × Fund, By Budget Unit, PD_Yearly_Summary, Curriculum_PD, PD by Subject).

Built with vanilla JS + Chart.js · No backend, no analytics · Source on GitHub
